# Session 1 {.unnumbered}

In this session, we build the foundation for everything that follows
in this course. We start by getting comfortable with VS Code and the
terminal, then move into creating and understanding the structure of
an oTree project, and finish with a hands-on exercise where you will
download, configure, and run a real oTree app.

**By the end of this session, you will be able to:**

- Navigate your file system using the terminal and create a new
oTree project and app from scratch.
- Read and make sense of what is inside an oTree project folder,
and understand the key components of the `settings.py` file.
- Download an app shared by someone else, add it to your project,
update `settings.py` to include it, and run a working demo in
your browser.

**This session has three parts:**

| Part | Title | What you will do |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| Getting Started | Getting Started: VS Code, Projects, and Apps | Learn the core terminal commands for working with oTree |
| Inside an oTree Project | Inside an oTree Project: Folder Structure and `settings.py` | Understand what oTree creates and how to configure it |
| Hands-On | Hands-On: Adding and Running Your First App | Download, configure, and demo a shared oTree app |
